author: SegmentLinking
body:
#### PR description:

Most LST objects (segments, triplets, quintuplets, ...) size their buffers from an upper-bound estimate computed in an initial counting pass before object creation (see previous PR's here: https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/50157, https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/48698, https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/47232). Memory for LST mini-doublets (two hits in a given module) are currently allocated using the minimum of a loose dynamic estimate (n hits lower sensor x n hits upper sensor) and static caps based on detector region. These static caps help reduce overallocation from the loose dynamic estimate but can cause truncation for high-occupancy events and have to be re-tuned by hand when hit selections or pT cuts change. This PR replaces these static caps with a precomputed exact-count: We first compute the number of mini-doublets that will be created in a counting pass and then allocate an exact-size buffer to store them. This eliminates possible truncation and reduces the memory allocated for mini-doublets for a small increase in overall timing. Mini-doublet memory drops from 31.4 MB  to 7.4 MB on average (~22% decrease in total memory/event for LST) for a ~7% increase in LST time/event.

c.c @slava77
title: LST: Remove Matrix Caps for MDs using Precompute
